headline: The Summit League's Silent Threat
storyline: NET #39, a 10-game winning streak, and an 11-seed. South Dakota State enters as the most statistically underseeded team in Regional 4 — not a Cinderella, a legitimate Sweet 16 threat. Their WAB ranks top-tier nationally and they arrive peaking at exactly the right time.

headline: A Top-25 Team Wearing a 10-Seed
storyline: NET #22 nationally — but Tennessee draws a 10-seed after going just 2-8 in their final stretch. The regular-season pedigree is real; the momentum is not. A cautionary tale for bracket builders expecting them to upset their way through.

headline: Closed the Season Flawless
storyline: The Sun Belt's automatic qualifier went 10-0 in their final stretch — the only 12-seed in this bracket to enter on a perfect closing run. JMU draws Kentucky, a 5-seed they would push hard if seeding tracked recent form.

headline: The MAAC's Best-Kept Secret
storyline: The highest NET-ranked 11-seed in this year's bracket at #53, Fairfield enters unbeaten in their last 10 games. Their matchup with Notre Dame is the most statistically mismatched 6-11 game of the first round.

headline: Top-25 NET Hidden in the 9-Line
storyline: A 9-seed carrying a NET rank of #23, USC brings one of the strongest résumés of any single-digit seed in the field. A first-round win would put a legitimately top-25 program in the Sweet 16 conversation immediately.

headline: Big 12 Champions — And Underseeded
storyline: West Virginia won the Big 12 Conference Tournament and enter as a 4-seed despite a NET rank of #18. Their WAB of 6.66 ranks #14 nationally. Against Miami (OH) to open — a team that should be sweating this matchup.

headline: MEAC Champions Close Perfect
storyline: Howard enters on a 10-game winning streak as MEAC tournament champions. At NET #156 they face long odds against Ohio State, but no 14-seed in this bracket arrives hotter. The definition of a team playing its best basketball heading into March.

headline: Unranked to Top-10: Only Team This Decade
storyline: Ohio State entered December receiving just one vote in the AP Poll. By February they were ranked #8 — the only team this season to start outside the top 25 and crack the top 10. A 3-seed with a WAB ranking in the top-8 nationally. History says teams with this kind of trajectory overperform their seed.

headline: Big Sky Perfect — 10-0 to Close
storyline: The Big Sky champion closed the regular season 10-0, the longest current winning streak among all 13-seeds. Idaho faces Oklahoma on the strength of a flawless second half — the definition of a team playing its best basketball.

headline: Dangerous Despite the Dip
storyline: A 3-seed coming off a 6-4 close, Louisville has the NET pedigree (#11) but not the momentum. Their opening matchup against Vermont figures to be tighter than the seed line suggests — this is a team that could make or miss a run depending entirely on whether their early-season form returns.

headline: Ivy League Elite in the 9-Line
storyline: AP #19 and NET #38, Princeton earned their way into the tournament as a 9-seed. Facing Oklahoma State to open, the Tigers bring a résumé that would be a 5-seed in a NET-seeded bracket. The matchup will be tight regardless of what the seed line says.

headline: ASUN's Hottest Team
storyline: NET #136 but winners of 9 of their last 10, Jacksonville earned their automatic bid on one of the longest active winning streaks among 15-seeds. A statistical long shot against LSU (#2 seed), but peaking at precisely the right time.
